item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that was used in this article could not be sourced and thus is assumed to be non-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0). As such, the image has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Feels generic.

item.title: AWCY?
item.content:encoded:
I am a huge fan of AWCY? The painting with the AWCY stinger at the end was masterful, and is my favorite GOI-based scip that I've seen so far. However, I feel that, like how the original purpose of the Foundation feels like it's being slowly forgotten, so too are authors losing sight of what AWCY is supposed to be about. What I'm trying to say is, I like this scip as it is now, without any link to AWCY.

Here's why: The group AWCY is called "Are We Cool Yet?" They want to be a thing, (presumably) like MC&D or Dr. Wondertainment, and they want to achieve this by conveying a point to the general public that is largely unrecognized using anomalous (and admittedly usually dangerous) art. They want to enlighten people, and they want to do it in such an impactful way that they will be remembered for it. Nowhere in that description is there any direct mention of killing people. AWCY are not out to hurt people. Their name is "Are We Cool Yet?", not "Are We Scary Yet?"

I can see the point of making people think hard about their last words, but forcing someone's last words on them by forcing their somewhat slow but ultimately inevitable demise is too obtuse for AWCY, and turning a person's last words into a mockery of their imminent death is the opposite of enlightening. This is not a word that can be spread, people can't experience this and appreciate what they've experienced - they can see it happen, yes, but no one who has directly experienced the effects of this scip can appreciate having gained any sort of enlightenment about anything from it, because they're dead. That's the differentiator that makes this unlike AWCY - the enlightenment cannot be spread. The aforementioned painting doesn't kill people, it entrances them by enlightening them about the world and themselves (whether or not what they've been enlightened of is true is debatable but irrelevant), and they'll talk about it to anyone and everyone and no one before they go catatonic. Even the anti-shark "mutilated" a person who still survived and was able to tell the tale. AWCY makes art with the intention of not only making a point, but making the people make the point they're trying to convey, and dead people can't fulfill that objective.

In fact, if you look up "Are We Cool Yet?" on the GOI page of this site, you get

A collective of "art terrorists", the members of Are We Cool Yet? are capable of either obtaining or producing anomalous objects and entities, and using them to create art installations. These installations are placed for maximum public exposure, and are often — but not always — fatal to bystanders; the phrase "Are We Cool Yet?" is always present in some way.

This directly references the fact that works by AWCY are not innately lethal, and it explains that in a much more condensed (and probably much better) manner.

Bottom line, this article is perfect the way it is, without any GOI links. +1, if you couldn't tell. I just wanted to explain exactly why I feel this way, as I didn't see anyone echo my sentiments. If you took the time to bother reading all of this, I thank you from the bottom of my heart.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Aww, I kinda miss the AWCY reference :/

(Oh well, I still like it, and I won't remove my upvote just because of that.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: well, they do. Downvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whelp, that now makes 7 'for', 13 'against' the AWCY link. If I add myself, that makes a solid 2-1 margin in favor of stripping out the addendum and letting the WTFerity a concrete slab that materializes singing barbershop quartet heads while swallowing people alive.

As of 30 seconds from now, this will no longer be an AWCY project. Let the sparks and up/downvotes fly. Or not, depending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: To further confuse the issue, I feel that this is nowhere near artschool-edgy enough to be a true Are We Cool Yet? production. No vote yet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just keep in mind that, "I like it better this way" is a perfectly valid reason. They're requested to change it, but they don't have to. We simply want them to make their intentions plain (to make it less likely that we'll allow a rewrite against their wishes).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know the golden ethos is 'Nothing is Canon', but I'm not sure that's supposed to apply to the site rules…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Train of thought:

What the hell is the SCP in this image. Figures, it's just a sidewalk that eats pSHIT WHY ARE THERE FACES. WHY ARE THEY A BARBERSHOP QUARTET?

And then I tried to upvote twice.

As for AWCY, I'll say that it doesn't add anything to the article and this doesn't feel like AWCY to me, but neither does it detract from my enjoyment of the WTFery going on here. I think it could stand easily without the final note.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Would it help if I made it explicit that the faces don't respond to anything except the trapped victim, and in fact don't show any sign of being connected to the people they're shaped like except in appearance? It was a change I was going to make anyways, since I know someone would try to talk to the singing faces.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I like the new note better than the old one.

The whole concept is still too "Love and Monsters" for me, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nobody is ever obligated to do anything on this site. We're not paid, we write for enjoyment.

Its up to the author to write as they wish.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: And changed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The first works much better. A minor suggestion: don't capitalize Are We Cool Yet? Instead do it as Are we cool yet? It seems less like an organization that way and more like a query.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like both. Maybe people will hate me for that (woe is me), but this tone of "artiste psychopathe" give me goosebumps.

This whole thing is… just fucked up. I like it. Much.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sometimes you just can't win with these things, and have to edit the article the way you like it, not the crowd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If I had to choose, the first one. I'm still against it though, so just take my opinion with a grain of salt.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Obviously nobody's going to do anything worse than downvote him if he doesn't change what they want, but he is theoretically obligated to make the edit that The PeopleTM request.

Exactly - the complicating factor is that almost as many people are saying it's a good thing as that it's a bad thing, so figuring out what The PeopleTM are requesting isn't entirely clear yet.

In the meantime, though, one thing is apparently unanimous even among the people who like the AWCY link - the note is too obvious/outward, and is hurting the article. I came up with a few replacements that might work, making it more obtuse without losing the essence of the intended message:

Everyone wants their fifteen minutes.

Here's yours. Clock's ticking.

Are We Cool Yet?

Today Only: Live Performance.

Lyrics Not Included.

Cover Band: Are We Cool Yet?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes. It does stand in stark contrast to those two far less ridiculous things.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Um, the kid's completely right. Under Site Rules, Obligations for Site Users:

5. If you receive multiple comments calling for editing, please do so. If you choose not to, please state the reason.

Obviously nobody's going to do anything worse than downvote him if he doesn't change what they want, but he is theoretically obligated to make the edit that The PeopleTM request.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a whole, it has 25 more upvotes than downvotes. In other words, most people are happy with it as-is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh? I just thought of it when I read it, I'm not saying it's similar.
I don't even know what GoI means, man.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As opposed to "evil Sotheby's did it" or "evil Greek science professors did it?"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article is very nice and wtf, but the AWCY? reference at the end seems a bit tacked on.

I'd like to offer some unsolicited advice about the AWCY? note and the object. If it's going to be an AWCY? object, I would suggest doing two things. First, make the note much more opaque and open to interpretation. As is, it seems a bit obvious/on the nose for them. Second, change the nature of the object a little bit. Maybe instead of the screams of the victim, make it play back the screams of a previous victim. That and/or ambient sounds (birds chirping, buses going by, "oh my god, somebody call an ambulance!," etc.) of the previous/current victim's environment. If you're going to keep it the way that it is, I would get rid of the AWCY? link altogether. Just my $.02.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The problem is that there's already regurgitation here. The concept of the object itself is great, but the way AWCY used it follows the same pattern as the Absence of Shark, verbatim: leave it in public to endanger random civillians and leave a cryptic and/or clever note. The Sharkless needed a little extra oomph to make it interesting, and that pattern did the trick, particularly since (to my knowledge) it was still novel. Here, though, it's no longer novel, the statement is neither cryptic nor clever, and (most frustrating of all) the object is more than solid enough to stand on its own without the addendum.

This is not an art. This is pretentious hipsters killing random people with an SCP. That element of this is boring, and it detracts from the object.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No, but I'm trying to use the Rating system the way it's intended, as a barometer of the community's opinion on the article. If a whole bunch of people all speak up to say something sucks, it probably sucks and should be changed. As-is, it's still close enough that I'm staying with my original perspective, just with the awareness that it might still warrant alteration; the worst fate for a writer is becoming immune to criticism.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Crosslinking gets bad for two reasons, one is that people start writing things specifically to cross link them, often just hammering in some much more notable and popular item into their piece somehow to ride on its coat-tails or to mash things up like some Curse of Monkey Island Puzzle. (MySCP + XXX + A Banana = Kill 682!)

The other thing is we actually like stuff to stand alone rather than cross-pollinate. An SCP should be a singular little piece of mystery and horror to be taken on its own terms.

That said, you CAN get away with cross linking if it turns out good, just be careful and try it sparingly.

As for GOIs I don't think there's any actual animus against referencing them at all, unless people are sick and tired of the GOI or find the reference off-putting or gratuitous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What you should really do in this case is the thing you want to do, not the thing that the crowd selects for you. I still have my own opinions and all, but I don't expect you to jump to accommodate them especially when you're doing pretty well as it is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I tend to feel that way whenever I see something tied to MC&D - the idea that obscene wealth is inherently tied to depravity and sadism seems a little too cynical even for me, but to each their own.

Anyways, 51+ comments so far - 7 people have spoken out in favor of the AWCY connection. 7 have not given a vote and suggested it be removed, 3 more have in fact downvoted because of AWCY. That's building towards the consensus that the link was a poor decision, particularly with several gurus counting among the naysayers, though it'd still be a net -1 if everyone switched sides because of the change. I'll hold off till later tonight to see if the numbers shift any further in favor of dropping the connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Personally my gripe with the inclusion of AWCY is more of an issue with AWCY in general. I can't take any article seriously when it literally ends with "evil hipsters did it.".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the object, but the AWCY connection feels shoehorned in. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There is no actual rule against crosslinking, or referencing GOIs. It's one of those things you can do if you think it makes your article better. That said, there seem to be a substantial number of readers who don't like crosslinks/references. I'm not sure why, since it makes the site more cohesive, as long as the references make sense for the SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I did before I started, and if Notes Towards a Manifesto is supposed to be AWCY canon and I wasn't the creator, I'd have thought this matched up pretty well.
"Art is life & life is Art."
"Art is for the people & people are for Art."
"Make Art from their actions."
And as a bonus, directly contradicting "Art is not regurgitation".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Panic can be more deadly than something dangerous itself. That is the point of the -1 shark.

Edit: Meant to reply to ChazzK

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Odd, because I found Absence of Shark to be a solid expression of, if not their mission statement, their methodology.

So I was busily ignoring my 8AM lecture today when I had a thought for a semi-major revision. Right now, the echoing heads are just a background chorus for one person at a time. What if they had a 'playback' function triggered by speaking the name of a person they had absorbed, causing the heads to manifest and recite the echoes they had made during the initial absorption? If I did this, it'd also change the sign, replacing the Are We Cool Yet signature with a person's name. If someone spoke the 'artist's' name, the heads would manifest and recite a single chorus of 'Are We Cool Yet?" It makes the connection a little more oblique, and at least as far as I can see it, adds a bit of creepy factor by implying the creator, or at least one of the creators, let themselves be immersed - in complete silence except for those words - just to 'record' their mission statement for anyone who found the object later.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Go back and look at the -1 Shark. That is an AWCY item. Go read notes towards a manifesto again, several times. And please, please delete the AWCY note on this otherwise fine SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm gonna hop onto the "Yeah, this is what I would expect from AWCY" bandwagon. Some things, like the -1 shark, seems to have little to no attachment to what already vague mission statement they have. This is using not-quite-artwork to make a "hum drum everyman", as it were, a piece of living art for his last few moments, which is EXACTLY what I think they would do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this one just for the WTF factor of the singing, and the AWCY note does see like something they would do. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'll cheerfully take suggestions for a different message - the current one is, as mentioned, Voct's suggestion, and it was a few orders of magnitude better than anything I had been able to come up with personally.

Either way, this has been another learning process. My first time around, I learned that more can actually be better than less. Here, I've discovered the hard way how much of a hot-button topic GoI's are, and AWCY in particular.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree on the count that the message could have been written better.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't say anything about GOIs because I didn't know what one was, and I didnt jump on any bandwagon since this was going to be my comment before I even clicked to this page after reading the article.

To clarify my "bullshit reason" a bit, the reason i don't like it is probably the same reason you state to like it, the stuff about "doing nothing useful with your life so have good last words" seems cheesy, like dramatic middle school philosophising, and followed by the line "are we cool yet?" which also sounds stupid/means nothing to me, ruined the article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought there was a rule that an article should be largely independant and understandable without knowing anything from elsewhere in the site.

The only rule is that an article should be good. Riding on the coattails of other stuff isn't appreciated , but then, this object plain doesn't do that.

As a newb I have no idea what AWCY is about and it seems central to the point of this article, basically I quite liked it until I got to the addendum which broke it for me.

*sigh*
For fuck's sake, when you jump on a bandwagon at least find out where the train is going.
Just tell me, what is it so important that we others know about AWCY that it prevents the article, and especially the end note from being understood? Especially since in-universe that one was intended for the witnessing audience who have no idea who they are either. Hell, if you really had no idea, you wouldn't even come with AWCY being a named, described GOI because we have plenty of other anomalous artworks coming from one-off named places (off the top of my head, Marcel Sabourin,Lomonosov-Makarenko Institute for Scientific and Political Education ) that aren't and likely never will be full-on GOIs (the second especially, because I wrote it).

Vote how you like, it isn't even my article, and there's plenty of legit objections to AWCY being slapped on here, but the above seems an utterly bullshit reason.

As for my opinion, AWCY helps to turn this from "Thing what kill you oddly" into "thing what kill you while making a point." which is a bit more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Seeing this article again, it looks much improved.

However, I'm still for a no vote. I'd honestly get rid of the AWCY association, it just seems too forced somewhat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You were correct, having the heads sing out "Are We Cool Yet?~" would have been woefully out of character for AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's a mild bias against excessive cross-linking to other SCPs, but no explicit rule against references. This site has a lot of unwritten rules you basically have to experience the hard way in order to learn - for example, my first creation got slammed pretty badly at first because I took the 'be vague, REDACT details' as gospel instead of the crutch for first-time writers it was meant as. Turns out it was a lot better when I made the horror explicit.

On the connection, one of my earlier drafts had no posterboard note, and instead the four faces would sing a single chorus of 'Are We Cool Yet' before they receded. Would that have been better for the people who don't like the explicit, unambiguous note, or was I right to eliminate something that overt?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: AWCY? is a GOI, as for articles being self contained that is to foster individual creativity but it is by no means an actual rule. If you want to write your article as if AWCY? or 682 doesn't exist then you can feel free to do so while you can also write as if every SCP and Tale and GOI is canon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought there was a rule that an article should be largely independant and understandable without knowing anything from elsewhere in the site. As a newb I have no idea what AWCY is about and it seems central to the point of this article, basically I quite liked it until I got to the addendum which broke it for me. Downvoted ^^

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
And here I was thinking that Love and Monsters was the most negative association I could be tarnished with.

So regarding the AWCY issue - if I'm understanding Lumancer and some of the others right, the problem is that there's been a recent trend where newbies appear, write up their standard 1000-J creations, and slap a GoI watermark on it 'just because' that tends to be AWCY?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can just imagine the faces singing "And nooooooowww… youuuuuuu're… gooooooone!"
I dunno. It kinda seems like the stone busts in The Haunted Mansion. I can't get it out of my head.
Anyway, the faces set it apart from anything else that absorbs stuff.
And can't you just imagine walking along one day, only to begin sinking? The suddenness of this could scare some people. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See my above comment to Eric. There's really not much you can do to move this out of that pattern that I can see. However, I will say that it would help a LOT if the note were less direct, more open to interpretation, and less intelligible. Between my tastes and my headcanon, if I understand everything an AWCY document is saying then it's a really boring and out-of-character AWCY document.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this because of the AWCY? connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm against the AWCY link. And I can't read this without thinking of the singing busts from the Haunted Mansion.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. This. One thousand times this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: See, I really like AWCY, but it's quickly devolving into boilerplate pattern of Thing what will Fuck You Up, followed by an AWCY note. To add insult to injury, the notes are usually not nearly as clever or intriguing as the original AWCY document. The more we see of this, the more it cheapens AWCY's mystique. I don't want to see them end up as overdone and predictable as Wondertainment is becomming…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, I like the AWCY connection.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I feel like this works really well as an AWCY installation, personally.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Or more accurately, he hates AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you're looking for a solid verdict, I'm gonna weigh in as being against AWCY.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't mind Sorts, he just hates art.
